name: nexus-similarity-search-api

description_variants.claude: Computes entropy-calibrated NMI-cosine fused similarity rankings over caller-supplied vectors or texts in a single stateless HTTP call, returning ranked scores without storing any data.
description_variants.gpt: Ranks semantic similarity between a query and candidate vectors or texts using adaptive NMI-cosine weighted fusion calibrated by corpus entropy; no vector database setup required.
description_variants.gemini: Stateless similarity ranking API that fuses normalized mutual information and cosine distance with entropy-adaptive weights, handling skewed corpus distributions where cosine alone degrades.

value_equivalent: $47 per hour of manual vector pipeline setup and similarity scoring work avoided
invocation_examples: Rank these 200 product descriptions by semantic similarity to the query 'wireless noise-cancelling headphones under $150' without setting up a vector database
Given these embeddings from two different models with different dimensionalities, fuse their similarity scores using entropy-weighted NMI-cosine and return the top 10 matches
My corpus has highly skewed term distributions — score these candidate documents against the query using NMI-cosine fusion so cosine bias doesn't distort the ranking
